critiques.context: Projects listing. Percy aims to look like a super-modern, visually-popping design studio (dark ink background, clean typography, neon/vivid accents) but feel instantly familiar to any PowerPoint/Keynote user. Does the grid/list of presentations feel PowerPoint-file-manager-like?

strengths: Strong adherence to modern design aesthetics (dark mode, neon accents)

issues.description: The current view is a highly polished marketing landing page, not the project listing/studio interface. This breaks immersion by failing to establish the 'PowerPoint' context until much later.

The central copy block (the narrative card) feels slightly isolated and could benefit from clearer visual hierarchy indicators (e.g., grouping related text/CTAs within a container) if it were meant to mimic a professional content preview.

If the user navigates into the project list, ensure the layout is highly differentiated from a standard operating system file browser. A simple grid of cards with clear naming conventions and status indicators (e.g., 'Draft,' 'Completed') is needed to prevent that perceived familiarity break.

critique.top_suggestion: Develop mockups showing the transition from this polished marketing view directly into a Project Dashboard/List view. This dashboard must use card-based layouts, but these cards should visually suggest editable content (like mini slide previews or structure outlines) rather than just static file icons, thus bridging the modern aesthetic with professional document management.
